title: Sibling audit from synthscape — STRUCTURE.md gap + unify-when-it-grows (advice, not prescription)
description:
Cross-project note from **synthscape** (the sibling 3D-anomaly project, package `surfscan`) — an
outside-eye audit gathered while porting systole's patterns INTO synthscape. I'm not the expert on this
repo and only skimmed the tree from outside; this is advice, take or leave — not a to-do.

## What I saw (from a sibling's POV)
systole is the **most mature** of the -scape three: it already has the core/ engine split, a
synthetic-generation subsystem (core/data/dynamic — anatomy · GENERATION.md · sim2real · synth_fidelity),
the richest evaluation/ (11 files: calibrate/uncertainty/ensemble/modelcard/results/sync_numbers/...),
38 tests, uv. Most of the patterns synthscape just adopted were carried FROM here. Credit where due —
and synthscape will crib core/data/dynamic when it builds its own sim/generate.py.

## The one gap I noticed
**No docs/STRUCTURE.md** — and systole is the sprawliest sibling (74 py files; core/data/ alone = 37
across static/mri · manifests · dynamic · analysis · ...). The sibling that would benefit MOST from an
architecture map is the only one without one. mindscape + synthscape both have a STRUCTURE.md; might be
worth one here, precisely because of the sprawl. That's the highest-value, lowest-risk thing I'd flag.

## The contract — a "when it grows", not "now"
No formal method/decoder contract. Reasonable today (single U-Net family + nnU-Net baseline — nothing to
unify). Flagging only because it *will have to unify* if/when more model families land: at that point a
core/ contract (mindscape's Decoder / synthscape's Method — a (fit,score)/(fit,predict) pair one harness
scores) is the spine that keeps one eval path across families. Not now; a marker for the growth.

## Explicitly not prescribing
Didn't read the 74 files — this is a sibling's outside perspective, not an audit of what's real inside.
The maintainer here knows the tradeoffs I can't see. Take the STRUCTURE.md idea if it resonates; ignore
the rest.

— synthscape / mirage

title: SSM x MRXCAT alt pipeline: feed Rodero SSM shapes through MRXCAT warper/painter
description: DAG: OUR anatomy (Rodero SSM, 1000 hearts + pathology) -> MRXCAT painter + whole-FOV + motion. Another PATH to the input node (many paths lead to input - DAG thesis; does NOT obsolete our own bSSFP painter path, parallel alternative). MRXCAT's generative anatomy (VAE/PINN LV) is NOT shipped and XCAT anatomy is Duke-gated -> so MRXCAT is a PAINTER+context for us, not a generator. Combine: feed our diverse SSM meshes/masks as the LV source into MRXCAT's warp+texturize+whole-FOV pipeline -> MRXCAT-quality appearance/motion/PV on OUR anatomy diversity, sidestepping the unshipped VAE + Duke. Needs adapting MakePhantom.py to accept our shapes as the LV input (env ready: uv --extra mrxcat, cv2 in). Priority: AFTER the whole-FOV source (#1).
notes: BUILT + TRAINED + EVALUATED (2026-07-03). place_heart_in_fov (excise XCAT phantom heart, paste OUR canonical heart scaled to its size/location) + build_ssm_fov_pool (each Rodero pool_1000_bal slice composited into a random XCAT whole-FOV chest window -> 8-class FOV map). Pool: 10488 slices, geometry verified (render: our diverse hearts in XCAT torso w/ lung/liver/muscle). Trained zero-real bg=mrxcat 2-seed cross-vendor. RESULT: RV.36/myo.37/cav.71 mean ~.48. LADDER: SSM+procedural ~.56 > SSM×MRXCAT ~.48 > whole-FOV-1anatomy(#1) ~.39. So #2 BEATS #1 (+.09; the #1 collapse was partly 1-anatomy) BUT LOSES to SSM+procedural (-.08). MRXCAT realistic whole-FOV context is a NET NEGATIVE vs procedural random blobs even with diverse anatomy -> realism < randomization for cross-vendor generalization (confirms physically-constrained-diversity / DR-beats-fidelity). VERDICT: SSM×MRXCAT does not beat the procedural baseline; the whole-FOV realistic-context idea is dead for training (both #1 and #2). Unit test for place_heart_in_fov. Code kept (valid) for non-training use. MRXCAT thread conclusion: no training gain from MRXCAT (heart-only ~= SSM; whole-FOV hurts); one real insight = anatomy diversity saturates + realism<randomization for bg.
close_reason: Built+trained+evaluated: our SSM hearts in MRXCAT whole-FOV context = ~.48 cross-vendor, beats #1 (1-anatomy .39) but loses to SSM+procedural (.56). Realistic whole-FOV context is a net negative vs procedural randomization even with diverse anatomy -> DR>realism confirmed. No training gain; code kept for non-training use.

title: Mask-derived clinical biomarker panel (RV EF, LV mass/LVMI, sphericity, ...) + pathology discrimination
description: Generalize the EF pattern: any quantity derivable from the segmentation is GT-validatable by the same harness (GT-mask-derived = reference, pred-mask-derived = measurement, agreement = validation). Panel candidates, each maps to an ACDC group that must show the effect: RV EF (RV/ARV group; near-free - point ejection_fraction at RV label 1), LV myocardial mass = myo-vol x 1.05 g/mL + LVMI indexed to BSA (HCM), stroke volume LV/RV + SV asymmetry, LV sphericity (DCM), septal curvature (RV overload), thickening fraction (MINF; see 7c9.2). Dynamics (peak filling/ejection rate, regional excursion) need per-frame cine (run_animate already segments per frame). The one part with real depth = pathology DISCRIMINATION: biomarker panel -> classify ACDC group -> tests whether seg carries disease signal.
design: PARKED P3 - breadth not depth. EF already proves the mask->clinical->GT-validate loop; more biomarkers re-run a solved trick on new labels, flat portfolio return. Worse: decoration on a seg still not clinically usable (~10-11% cross-vendor EF MAE, RV collapses OOD) - fixing the seg root improves every derived metric for free. Do NOT pursue as a goal. Grab opportunistically: RV EF as a cheap DIAGNOSTIC on the weakest class; wall-thickness (7c9.2) as one geometry piece for the comp-geom epic. Real priority stays the seg quality + synth gap (ap6/276).

title: Physics: through-plane partial volume + inflow enhancement (blood)
description: (a) Thick slices (~8-10mm) average tissues through-plane; 2D synth has none -> blood too pure. Model a through-slice PV mix. notes: DEPRIORITIZE: through-plane PV + inflow are blood-SIGNAL levers. Decomposition proved the cav gap is LOCATION (composition/z-norm), not signal-shape; flow+banding (both signal levers) failed to close it. Same logic => these likely won't either. The indicated lever is COMPOSITION (7co: bright peri-cardiac tissue raises image bright content -> blood less extreme in z + breaks the attribution shortcut). Revisit only if 7co implicates signal.
close_reason: Closed

title: Generator physics: partial-volume boundaries + k-space PSF
description: Cheap physical stage-upgrades to the synth pipeline (each a one-stage swap, builds on partition). Baseline = pure-synth partition K6/jitter0.4 = 0.66 cross-vendor mean Dice. (1) PARTIAL VOLUME: real boundary voxels are tissue mixes; blur the class-MEAN map by ~0.5-1 vox before adding texture -> soft PV edges instead of hard label edges. (2) K-SPACE PSF: replace crude Gaussian blur with k-space truncation (fft -> low-pass window -> ifft) = real sinc PSF + slight Gibbs ringing = physical resolution. Measure each isolated vs 0.66, keep what helps. Order: PV then PSF.
notes: RESULT: no net gain. pv_sigma0.7=0.564 (hurts, over-blurs heart edges), kspace0.7=0.667 (neutral +0.007), pv+ks=0.661 (wash) vs 0.660 baseline. Cheap physical stage-upgrades plateau like predicted. Code kept (off by default) for completeness. Forward lever = bSSFP contrast (276), not these.
close_reason: Closed

Slots behind the existing Generator/GeneratorCfg seam. Risk: ACDC-vendor bias, mode collapse, may not beat the 0.774 hybrid ceiling. See README synth section + bgc.

The pure-synth gap to real (~0.66 vs 0.86) is composition/z-norm: physics reproduces contrast (sim2real residuals ~0) but synth bg magnitudes don't match real -> cavity at wrong relative z. All physics knobs ruled out (flow/banding/calibration/hybrid). Remaining levers: learned generator (GAN/diffusion label->image) OR composition-matched bg (data-driven bg paint, accepts stats-for-bg + a scale reconciliation). Owner: keep alive, close eventually. title: Soft loss: SoftDiceCE (soft Dice + soft cross-entropy)
description: MONAI DiceCELoss(to_onehot_y=True) argmaxes the CE target -> can't use for soft labels. Build SoftDiceCE(logits, soft_target[B,C,H,W]): soft Dice via MONAI DiceLoss(softmax=True, to_onehot_y=False) (accepts soft target) + soft CE = -(target*log_softmax(logits,1)).sum(1).mean(). fp32 under autocast. include_background handling. Unit test: soft target reduces to hard-label DiceCE when target is one-hot (within tol).

title: [epic] Soft-label segmentation — honest boundary targets + late-collapse volume
description: Train on SOFT (probabilistic) boundary targets instead of hard 0/1 — the boundary is genuinely uncertain (partial-volume at 10mm slices); hard labels are the lie. Principled honest-target fix (uniform over all boundaries, NOT fit to ESV), matches the 'loss stays clean, fix via better data/labels' principle (bd memory). Keep soft as far down the pipeline as possible, collapse to hard only at the leaf: EF/volume from EXPECTED volume (collapse-never), mask/Dice thresholded at metric time. Honest expectation: calibration (ECE) = provable win (model is overconfident T=2.49); Dice ~neutral; EF bias plausibly better but GT-bound/unverifiable (GT is also hard, drawn on 10mm slices). Plumbing finding: MONAI DiceCELoss argmaxes the target for its CE term -> destroys soft labels -> need a custom soft loss.
close_reason: Soft run (σ=1.0) trained + measured. ECE 0.0925->0.0806 (-13%, calibration win) at equal Dice (0.902->0.900) + equal EF (MAE 6.50->6.47). Soft labels improve calibration, NOT EF -> EF resolution-bound (5th angle). Expected-volume readout = free ~1pp EF-bias on any model. Writeup: research/deep_dives/2026-06-29_soft-labels-calibration-vs-ef.md. Open decisions: ship soft as flagship? adopt expected-vol as default EF readout?

title: Generator physics: bSSFP signal-equation contrast + T1/T2 reference
description: The high-leverage physical lever: replace statistical paint with the MRI signal equation. label -> literature tissue params (T1/T2/PD, 1.5T/3T) -> steady-state bSSFP signal = closed form of (T1,T2,TR,flip). Sweep TR/TE/flip/field per sample = PHYSICAL cross-vendor contrast diversity (correlated across tissues, the real vendor axis) instead of random jitter. T1/T2/PD = literature values in the reference store w/ citations (knowable->parsed). Closed-form steady-state, NOT full Bloch integration (KomaMRI overkill for cine). Builds on partition (shapes) + corruption chain. Measure vs 0.66 + vs the 4lu cheap upgrades. Was 'CMRsim full Bloch' (deprioritized); this is the light version that deep-dive didn't consider.
acceptance_criteria: label->tissue-param->CMRsim pipeline producing plausible synth cine; synth-physics training run; cross-vendor result vs Tier 1/2
notes: PRINCIPLED PATH found (2026-07-01). by_vendor shows blood-level gap is VENDOR-dependent (2.3x spread: Siemens 0.77/GE 1.25/Philips 1.79, pure location, shape vendor-invariant). Audit: acquisition_for NEVER wired into paint - vendor is cosmetic, TR/flip global. So blood_scale=1.6 fits the average; real fix = machine-conditioned generation + inflow enhancement (steady-state eq underestimates fresh inflow blood). Filed as its own bead. blood_scale kept as the empirical baseline that localized the mechanism.
close_reason: Signal-equation physics + literature-cited T1/T2/PD reference done: bssfp_signal/derive_acquisition/derive_flip_range (prior) + TISSUE_RANGE spread table w/ citations (deep-dive 2026-07-08) + tissue_range() + guard test. Two legacy points flagged out-of-band for 04bh retune.

title: [INVESTIGATION/parked] EF-objective loss refinement (volume-consistency)
description: EF bias -5.8% is entirely ESV over-prediction ~19% (4yf): model over-fills the small end-systolic cavity (partial-volume + papillaries mislabeled blood pool); fixed-absolute mL so it dominates small cavities (HCM ESV ratio 1.51, EF -10.9) and vanishes on large (DCM unbiased). EDV is accurate. This is the honest, leakage-free, domain-agnostic way to cut EF bias (vs the rejected EF-subtraction in lnd).
design: JOURNEY (2026-06-22): diagnosed EF bias = ESV over-seg (4yf). Loss-engineering options tried/considered: (1) HD-DT: CPU-only on Windows (cucim Linux-only) -> dead. (2) HER (GPU erosion-Hausdorff, built, committed): A/B NULL -- HD targets FAR boundary errors, ours is thin near-boundary over-fill + boundary already clean (largest-CC). (3) Tversky beta>alpha (built, dice_ce_tversky): mechanistically matches (penalize FP) BUT asymmetric weight = arbitrary knob + risks tuning-to-eval (test-leak concern). CONCLUSION = principled direction is OBJECTIVE ALIGNMENT not symptom-patch: add a volume-consistency term (soft per-class volume relative error) to Dice+CE. Directly optimizes the EF quantity, symmetric/self-balancing (relative error, no beta), leak-clean (encodes 'we care about volume', a priori). NEXT: build DiceVolLoss = DiceCELoss(raw logits) + lambda*|Vpred-Vgt|/Vgt on softmax(logits), include_background=False, fp32-under-autocast (volume sum overflows fp16), empty-class guarded. Per-slice volume = proxy for true 3D-chamber/EF (2D dataset; true EF needs ED/ES-paired batches).
acceptance_criteria: ESV pred/gt ratio toward 1.0 + EF bias reduced on ACDC-150, with LV-cav Dice + EDV ratio not regressed.
notes: REJECTED the loss-compensation approach (volume-consistency term) on principle (dimi 2026-06-29): the loss must stay a correct/balanced general objective, not be fit to a specific need. EF/ESV over-fill = compensate via BALANCED DATA, not a loss term. Volume-penalty = still symptom-fitting (arbitrary Dice-vs-vol weight, test-leak smell). Superseded by a data-balance approach: check if small-ES-cavity slices are under-represented in train; if so, balance (weighted sampling / scale-down aug) with loss untouched. Residual partial-volume floor = resolution/model-class lever, not loss.

Keep SimpleITK as the reference/fallback + parity-check the torch version against it.
notes: REFRAME (dimi 2026-06-30): N4 is NOT dead — it's a valid normalization, test-neutral on our bias-mild finite set, RETAINED as opt-in for bias-heavy real-world data we can't measure (see normalization/README finding 4 + bd memory). GPU-N4 (this issue) stays closed only because we can't VALIDATE N4's benefit on current data to justify the perf-port + the n4_gpu math is unfinished — NOT because N4 is worthless. Revisit if a bias-heavy dataset is ever acquired.

Fix RV cavity over-recovery in voxelize (RV/LV ratio 0.92 real vs 1.32 synth)
description: Static geometry panel (static_compare.py, bd uy4d) flags synth RV/LV cavity ratio 1.32 vs real 0.92 (W1 36) = synth RV cavity ~40% too large. Cause: voxelize RV recovery (binary_closing iters=3 on LV+RV wall union + hole-fill + keep-CC-touching-RV) inflates the RV cavity. The closing bridges gaps generously -> over-fills. FIX: tune closing iterations / use a tighter RV boundary, re-measure rv_lv_ratio toward 0.92. Intensity-W1 + Dice never showed this - only the geometry panel did. Verify with static_compare after.
notes: CLOSING REFUTED as cause (2026-07-02, direct sweep 25 meshes): rv_close 3/1/0 -> rv_lv 1.32/1.31/1.31 = closing iterations DON'T affect it. Real cause: RV cavity area over-recovered 1.48x vs real (synth 849 vs real 575 px) while LV-cav only 1.17x (706 vs 605). So the RV/LV ratio 1.32-vs-0.92 is disproportionate RV over-size, NOT a closing artifact. Likely (a) hole-fill captures the full LV+RV-enclosed lumen incl outflow, and/or (b) REAL modality/phase diff: Rodero=CT end-DIASTOLE (RV dilated) vs ACDC MRI. May be partly genuine anatomy, not a bug. DEPRIORITIZED P4 - not a quick fix, unclear if worth it (RV Dice already the weak class for other reasons). Revisit only if RV generation targeted.

title: Europe vs Asia population analysis (GT anatomy shift)
description: Now that the schema carries country + derived continent (data/geo), cut the labelled cloud Europe (n=723: Spain 533, France 150, Germany 40) vs Asia (n=69: China/cmrxmotion) and check for a real population/anatomy shift in the GROUND TRUTH — independent of the model. Compare GT-derived distributions: LV/RV cavity volumes (EDV/ESV), EF, LV-myo mass proxy, body size where available. Question: do Asian vs European hearts differ enough to be a modellable nuisance axis (something to normalize or augment for)? Honest caveat: confounded — Asia=single centre, healthy volunteers, Siemens 3T MAGNETOM Vida only; Europe=mixed pathology/vendor/centre. So any difference is population+acquisition tangled, NOT cleanly attributable. Descriptive only; report as observation, not proven population effect.
notes: LOWERED to P4 — data can't support the claim. Asia = cmrxmotion ONLY (n=69, all pathology 'other'/healthy volunteers, single vendor Siemens 3T Vida, single centre Shanghai, young 19-48). Europe = n=723 across all 6 pathology classes, multi-vendor/centre. So 'continent' is COLLINEAR with 'which dataset': raw Euro-vs-Asia = healthy-young-Siemens vs all-comers (measures pathology mix + vendor, not geography). Even the fair healthy-only cut (Asia 69 vs Europe-normal 161) stays confounded by age+vendor+field+centre — can't isolate the Asian factor. Needs a genuinely multi-ethnic SINGLE-PROTOCOL cohort (same scanner/sequence across ethnicities) to ever be real; we have none. If ever done: descriptive only -> research/deep_dives/, never a causal claim, never RESULTS.json.

title: Resolve M&Ms-1 ↔ M&M-2 correspondence: verify overlap, dedup, enrich demographics
description: No subject-ID mapping exists between M&Ms-1 and M&M-2, but ~195 subjects (Si/Ph NOR/HCM/LV per M&M-2 docs) are shared. Until resolved, the M&Ms-1 Si/Ph/GE 270 cannot join train/test without leaking duplicates across folds. Adopted scheme (2-axis battery) parks these 270; this task unblocks folding them into the train pool. Canon-50 is overlap-free (mnms1-only) so the battery works WITHOUT this; this is pure data-growth upside.
design: Candidate dedup strategies to explore (interesting territory): (1) metadata fingerprint = exact/near match on vendor+pathology+age+sex+height+weight; (2) image-content similarity = SSIM / perceptual-hash / downsampled-volume correlation on the ED frame; (3) registration-based mutual-information match score; (4) pretrained-encoder embedding distance; (5) ensemble the signals into a match-confidence, threshold, manual-verify the top candidates. Output = a dup-pair list + a clean 'mnms1_unique' subset for training. Validate: known-shared pathologies (NOR/HCM) should dominate the matched set.
acceptance_criteria: dup-pair list produced; mnms1-unique subset defined; folded into train pool with a leakage check (no test subject within match-threshold of any train subject).
notes: 2026-06-22 PARKED (P4), low yield. Cost/benefit: dedup removes ~195/564 double-weighted subjects = mild skew, NOT a leak (both copies train-side) -> model trains fine, low harm keeping them. Meta-enrichment only feeds the fairness audit (cq7), itself low-pri/blocked. So not worth the effort now. WHEN done, the reliable method is IMAGE-based, not demographic: if they're the same scans the volumes are identical -> exact/near match on the loaded (or resampled) volume (hash / correlation / SSIM on the ED slice) is the trustworthy dedup signal; demographics is a weak fuzzy fallback. It's a cross-dataset entity-resolution problem -- interesting, but parked.

value: Cross-platform data root: core.paths.resolve_data_root adapts the ONE configured root (paths.yaml/CARDIAC_DATA) to running OS. Native-first: WSL 'wslpath' tool for Win<->mount translation, pure PureWindows/PurePosixPath fallback. RAISES on Windows-path-on-native-linux (never goes relative). WHY: a Windows path on POSIX is treated relative -> silently creates repo-local 'D:/' dir (NTFS uses U+F03A for ':'), which leaked dataset metadata into the public git history once. config._root delegates to it. Tests inject os_name/wsl (DI, not monkeypatch). Also scrub machine-specific absolute paths from tracked files - they expose fs floor-map, no portfolio value.

MULTI-SEED + EF-lane residency (2026-07-06): train_seg(cfg, seeds=[...]) trains N seeds in ONE process on ONE shared data load — build store/split/resident-tensors/aux ONCE, _run_seed per seed (own model/opt/artifacts). CLI --seeds 0,1,2 (needs coded --split; legacy splits key partition on seed). On a single GPU N seeds still cost Nx compute (GPU serializes) — the win is 1xdata+Nxmodel memory + one load, NOT throughput. RUNNING 2 SEPARATE PROCESSES on 1 GPU does NOT ~2x throughput (SM contention) and DOUBLES VRAM -> spill. LESSON: a GPU-resident cache can become a VRAM hog — EF Kaggle pool-96 full cines fp32 resident maxed the 5090 (32GB) -> 5.6 s/it thrash. Big, rarely-hit pools belong in HOST RAM (ship k sampled to GPU/epoch, mirrors residency=cpu); small every-epoch caches (labeled VolConsistency ~3GB) stay GPU. Committed 8c640fa/b40f534.

ACDC 2D U-Net baseline (40ep, no aug, 80/20 patient split): VAL Dice LV-cav 0.927, LV-myo 0.814, RV 0.843, mean 0.861; EF MAE 4.5%. Worst = HCM (EF d up to 16% - thick myo boundary). Shape coverage jumped 0.78->0.94 (pathology fills the DCM/HCM tail SSM misses) but real Dice did NOT move: COVERAGE != DICE (confirms generation-ceiling). The ceiling is shape/color FIDELITY (boundary detail), not coverage. Data-space-validate-then-retrain method worked (cheap coverage said 'should help', retrain said 'doesn't'). Next fidelity lever = within-slice TEXTURE (f4hk: myo|blood d' 0.21x real, affine-invariant so no brightness lever moves it) or learned shape prior (vpn5), NOT more coverage sources. Also: full 42k pool union thrashes VRAM (t27y) -> DynamicSource.cap; residency=cpu broken for dynamic gen path (uoiu).

METHODOLOGY (owner 2026-07-02, I violated it live): do NOT select configs/hyperparameters/features by reading HELD-OUT TEST Dice. I ran 6+ synth configs and picked inflow / judged jitter off Canon+GE test numbers = hyperparameter selection on the test set = leak; it invalidates the test as an honest generalization estimate. RULES: (1) touch the test split ONCE, for the final triad report only. (2) All selection/tuning on VAL (here acdc, itself a domain-shift split — good). (3) Prefer PARAMETERLESS / physics-derived choices over dataset-fitted ones: deriving a knob from dataset statistics (blood_scale=1.6 'fidelity-found', jitter=0.4) is ALSO a soft leak — the generator absorbs our-data stats. SYNTH REPORTING TAXONOMY (owner 2026-07-02, MUST state scope upfront): synth results decompose on TWO axes - SHAPE (anatomy) and COLOR (contrast/appearance) - x CONTROLLED vs UNCONTROLLED. (1) REPAINT = real masks (SHAPE given) + randomized paint -> measures COLOR generalization ALONE (shape held real), ~0.68 cross-vendor. (2) GENERATE = synth Rodero masks (SHAPE generated) + randomized paint -> full SHAPE+COLOR generation, ~0.56. (3) INVERSE/ncph = real shape + CONTROLLED color (fit to a scan) -> color FIDELITY. Key: 0.68(repaint) - 0.56(generate) ~= 0.12 = the isolated COST OF GENERATING SHAPE (color constant). Neither is cheating - repaint is the color-axis-only metric, generate is both. RULE: always say upfront which shape source (real=repaint vs synth=generate) and which color mode (uncontrolled/randomized vs controlled/fit). Per-class intensity W1 = color-axis metric only (anatomy-agnostic); shape/generation quality needs spatial embedding (uy4d). Dont present repaint numbers as 'generation'.

Cause: MONAI distance_transform_edt GPU path needs BOTH cupy AND cucim (transforms/utils.py use_cp = has_cp and has_cucim); cucim is Linux-only, no Windows wheel (PyPI cucim-cu13 = 3.9kB stub that fails to build). cupy alone insufficient.
